content: Curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM, RND) can be augmented by working memory retention gating mechanisms to prioritize retaining only the surprise signals that drive consistent intrinsic exploration progress, rather than encoding every unpredictable environmental observation as a novelty reward. This gated curiosity framework would automatically discard noisy or trivial novelty spikes that derail exploration, while preserving high-value unexpected patterns that lead to long-term task discovery.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
